hypothesis: This strategy uses Fibonacci levels and ATR to identify periods of high volatility in the market, then combines these with common inputs such as RSI, EMA, and VWAP for trade entry. The stop loss is set at 1.5 times the ATR below the trade entry point and take profit at three times the ATR above it. This strategy uses an average true range (ATR) based risk management system and checks that both momentum oscillators RSI and ADX are showing bullish or bearish trends, respectively.
Combining Fibonacci levels with ATR and OBV for identifying accumulation/distribution periods in market volatility to enter LONG/SHORT trades.
